title: EventQueue destructive __iter__ violates principle of least surprise
description: events.py EventQueue.__iter__ pops all events, emptying the queue. Standard Python iterators don't modify collections. Contributors debugging with 'for e in queue: print(e)' will accidentally empty it. Consider rename to drain() or make non-destructive.
close_reason: Made EventQueue.__iter__ non-destructive (yields events in time order via sorted snapshot, leaves queue intact) and added an explicit drain() for the consume-while-iterating use case. No production code relied on the old destructive behavior. Tests updated: test_iterate_is_non_destructive + test_drain_pops_all_events (events.py, tests/sim/test_events.py).
